question_text: Two astronauts have the same mass, but one is standing on the Moon (where g ≈ 1.6 N/kg) and one is on Earth (where g = 10 N/kg). Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: They have the same weight because they have the same mass.

options.label: B
options.text: The astronaut on Earth has a greater weight because Earth has a stronger gravitational field.

options.label: C
options.text: The astronaut on the Moon has a greater weight because the Moon is smaller.

options.label: D
options.text: Their weights are the same, but their masses are different.

explanation: Weight depends on both mass and gravitational field strength (W = m × g). Although both astronauts have the same mass, Earth's gravitational field (10 N/kg) is stronger than the Moon's (1.6 N/kg), so the astronaut on Earth weighs more.
